by_session.2024-12_03: Travelling in space has long been a dream of the Chinese nation. In 2003, the Shenzhou V spacecraft was launched successfully, and Yang Liwei became the first Chinese astronaut to enter space. In 2008, Shenzhou VII was launched, and Zhai Zhigang became the first Chinese astronaut to conduct a spacewalk in history. In recent years, China's aerospace industry has entered a fast lane of innovative development, with steady progress in space infrastructure construction, and the Chinese space station was fully completed in 2022. The rapid development of China's aerospace industry has written a glorious chapter in the history of the Chinese nation and made great contributions to the progress of human civilization. In the future, China's steps in exploring space will become steadier and go farther.
by_session.2024-12_02: Yangshan Port is an important part of the Shanghai international shipping centre. It is China's first deep-water port and one of the largest deep-water ports in the world. After nearly 20 years of development, Yangshan Port has achieved a high degree of automation. The use of digital technology and artificial intelligence has greatly reduced labour costs and carbon emissions. The independently developed terminal management system enables remote control of large equipment from hundreds of kilometres away. Yangshan Port looks busy, yet no manual operation is seen on site, and it can operate around the clock without interruption. Yangshan Port will continue to develop and make greater contributions to building Shanghai into a global shipping centre.
by_session.2024-12_01: The successful development of the BeiDou Navigation Satellite System is a major scientific and technological achievement China has made since reform and opening up. After relentless efforts, researchers overcame a series of technical challenges, and the BeiDou system finally achieved global coverage and high-precision positioning, making China one of the few countries in the world that independently owns a global satellite navigation system. The BeiDou system has been widely applied in fields such as transportation, disaster relief, weather forecasting and public security. It is now widely recognized internationally and is beginning to provide high-quality services to more and more countries and regions.
by_session.2024-06_02: China abounds in bamboo and is the earliest country to develop and utilize bamboo resources. Bamboo is widely distributed in China and rich in varieties. It is highly practical and used in many aspects of production and daily life, such as making chopsticks, tables and chairs, as well as building bridges and houses. Chinese people love bamboo. Since ancient times, countless men of letters have created splendid literary and artistic works with bamboo as the theme. The straight stem of bamboo symbolizes an upright character. Bamboo has strong vitality and adaptability and can survive tenaciously no matter how harsh the environment is, thus implying a spirit of perseverance. For thousands of years, bamboo has been regarded as a symbol of the character of the Chinese nation.
by_session.2024-06_01: China's traditional wedding customs have a long history. A complete set of wedding ceremonies gradually took shape as early as the Zhou Dynasty, and some of them are still in use today. Although Chinese wedding customs have changed greatly nowadays, the wedding celebration remains a grand occasion. The wedding venue is elaborately decorated, with red — the colour symbolizing jubilance — as the main tone, and many objects expressing wishes for the happiness of the newly-weds are displayed. At the wedding, the bride and groom bow to Heaven and Earth, bow to their parents, and bow to each other; then they hold a banquet to entertain the guests and propose toasts to thank them. Today, many young people are still fond of traditional Chinese weddings, experiencing the unique and wonderful romance of the Chinese style.
by_session.2023-12_03: In recent years,China's elderly population has continued to grow. The Chinese government is taking various measures to promote the construction of a service system for the elderly,so that the elderly can live a healthy andhappy life in their later years. Inorder to improve the service quality of elderly care institutions,the government has promulgated a list of standards to strengthen the supervision of elderly care. In order to make it easier for the elderly to eat,many cities have opened community canteens to provide them with affordable meals. Meals on wheels are also available forthe elderly with limited mobility. At the same time,China is also activelyexploring other old-age care models,such as home-based and communityold-age care,to ensure that all elderly people have adequate old-agesupport.
by_session.2023-12_02: With the development of economy and society,China's population structurehas undergone significant changes and gradually entered the aging society. China's elderly population will continue to increase,and the trend ofpopulation aging will become more obvious. In order to cope with thechallenges brought by the aging population,the country is actively takingmeasures to increase support for the elderly. Through the reform of the socialsecurity system,the government has continuously increased social securityfunding and gradually expanded the coverage of social security to benefitmore elderly people. Associations were also encouraged to provide servicesfor the elderly. With the joint efforts of the government and social groups,wewill not live a happier life.
by_session.2023-12_01: In China, with the arrival of an aging society, elderly care has received widespread attention. What people talk about most is what kind of elderly care model should be adopted. Most people believe that the elderly care model needs to be diversified. Through government guidance and social participation, more and better elderly care service institutions can be established, community service centres can be improved, home-based self-care for the elderly can be encouraged, and a model combining family care with social care can be promoted. As the government and society continue to increase investment in elderly care services, elderly care facilities are constantly upgraded and service quality is gradually improved. The lives of the elderly will become more convenient, comfortable, healthy and happy.
by_session.2020-12_02: The Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge, with a total length of 55 kilometres, is an extraordinary engineering feat of China. Connecting the three cities, it is the longest sea-crossing bridge-and-tunnel system in the world. The bridge has shortened the travel time among the three cities from three hours to 30 minutes. This reinforced-concrete bridge with a huge span fully demonstrates that China is capable of building record-breaking gigantic structures. It will boost regional integration and promote economic growth. The bridge is the key to China's master plan for developing its own Greater Bay Area. China hopes to build the Greater Bay Area into a region comparable to the bay areas of San Francisco, New York and Tokyo in terms of technological innovation and economic prosperity.
by_session.2019-12_02: The plum blossom, ranking first among the top ten famous flowers in China, originated in southern China and has a cultivation history of over three thousand years. In the depth of winter, colourful plum blossoms brave the severe cold and bloom proudly against the wind and snow. In traditional Chinese culture, the plum blossom symbolizes strength, purity and elegance, inspiring people to forge ahead in spite of difficulties. Since ancient times, many poets and painters have drawn inspiration from the plum blossom and created countless immortal works. Ordinary people also love plum blossoms, which are often used for home decoration during the Spring Festival. The city of Nanjing has designated the plum blossom as its city flower and holds a plum blossom festival every year, when thousands of people brave the severe cold to enjoy the blossoms in the snow on Meihua Mountain.
by_session.2025-12_01: Respecting the elderly is a traditional virtue of the Chinese nation, deeply rooted in the thoughts and behaviors of Chinese people, and it is a universally observed code of conduct and social norm. In today's China, this virtue has been widely carried forward. All sectors of society actively foster a social atmosphere of respecting and assisting the elderly. Providing convenient services for the elderly has become a social consensus, as seen in the establishment of canteens for the elderly in communities and the provision of priority seats in public places. The government has also introduced a series of policies to offer strong protection for the rights and interests of the elderly. Respecting the elderly serves as an important cornerstone for social harmony and development, helping to cultivate a positive social atmosphere and promote the overall progress of civilization in society.
by_session.2025-12_02: The Chinese nation advocates the spirit of self-reliance and self-improvement, which is deeply rooted in Chinese culture and serves as a vital pillar for the nation's survival and development. Since the reform and opening up, China has continuously increased its investment in scientific research on its journey of self-reliance, achieving new breakthroughs in fields such as information technology, artificial intelligence, and aerospace engineering. 5G networks now cover the entire country, telemedicine has developed rapidly, the use of electronic payments leads the world, and the Chang'e lunar exploration program has demonstrated remarkable progress in the aerospace sector. The spirit of self-reliance and self-independence has become a powerful driving force for the nation's modernization process, inspiring the Chinese people to strive unceasingly for the realization of the Chinese Dream of national rejuvenation.
by_session.2025-12_03: Frugality is a traditional virtue of the Chinese nation. In ancient China, when productivity was low, the ancestors were keenly aware that the fruits of labor were hard-won, and therefore adhered to the principle of frugality. Today, despite material abundance, the Chinese people still maintain a frugal lifestyle, which embodies a rational concept of consumption and respect for labor. In recent years, the Chinese government has continuously strengthened the building of a conservation-minded society, advocating the "Clean Your Plate Campaign" to reduce food waste, promoting a simple and low-carbon lifestyle, advancing resource recycling, and opposing excessive consumption. The building of a conservation-minded society has effectively propelled the high-quality development of China's economy.
by_session.2025-06_03: The Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area enjoys a unique geographical location, well-developed transportation infrastructure, and abundant industrial resources. As one of China's most open and economically vibrant regions, it holds a strategically important position in the country's economic development. The Greater Bay Area is not only expected to become a dynamic world-class city cluster and a global center for technological innovation, but also a high-quality living circle suitable for work, shopping, and tourism. With the continuous deepening of reform and opening up, the construction of the Greater Bay Area will further promote regional economic development. By 2035, the Greater Bay Area will achieve the goal of becoming a world-class bay area.
by_session.2025-06_02: Since ancient times, China's water resources have been scarce in the north and abundant in the south, with an extremely uneven distribution. To effectively address the severe water shortage in the north, the Chinese government has implemented the South-to-North Water Diversion Project. This is a massive water conservancy project that allocates water resources across regions. After decades of planning and preparation, the project commenced construction in 2002. It consists of three routes—eastern, central, and western—with a total length of 4,350 kilometers, benefiting over 400 million people. Since it began water diversion in 2014, the project has cumulatively transferred more than 50 billion cubic meters of water to the north, providing a reliable water source for the people in the northern region, significantly improving the ecological environment there, and promoting sustainable and healthy economic development.
by_session.2025-06_01: The Tiangong Space Station, China's first space laboratory, has a usable space of over 110 cubic meters, can accommodate three astronauts, and operates in an orbit 400–450 kilometers above the Earth's surface. It has carried out more than 180 scientific research and application projects, covering fields such as space life sciences, space medicine, and space materials science. The research results from the Tiangong Space Station have been widely applied in China, generating significant economic benefits. For instance, space breeding alone has created direct economic benefits amounting to hundreds of billions of yuan. This not only marks China's tremendous progress in space technology, but also demonstrates China's significant contribution to global space research and applications.
by_session.2024-06_03: Fans have been deeply loved by Chinese people since ancient times, but now they are no longer merely tools for cooling off in summer; instead, they are increasingly appreciated as works of art. Many fans are beautifully shaped and exquisitely crafted, adorned with fine patterns of landscapes, flowers, birds, and figures, thus possessing high artistic value. Numerous renowned Chinese painters and calligraphers enjoy writing poems and painting on fans to showcase their artistic taste. Fans are often given as gifts to convey good wishes and sincere emotions. Today, the practical function of fans has greatly diminished, but as a cultural symbol and art form, they still play a significant role in traditional Chinese culture.
by_session.2023-06_02: In recent years, an increasing number of Chinese cultural products have entered the global market and are increasingly favored by overseas consumers. With the rapid development of China's foreign cultural trade, the export volume of Chinese cultural products has remained among the top in the world for many consecutive years, giving rise to a group of culturally influential enterprises, products, and brands. According to data, the overseas sales of Chinese publications, films and television works, online literature, and animation have been rising year after year. The Chinese government has introduced a series of policies to encourage and support more outstanding cultural products with Chinese elements to go global, expand their overseas market share, and further enhance the global influence of Chinese culture.
by_session.2023-06_03: In recent years, Chinese cities have accelerated their development, leading to significant improvements in the urban living environment. Many cities are actively exploring a path of high-quality urban development with Chinese characteristics, with urban functions being continuously enhanced and governance levels markedly raised. China has persistently pursued urban ecological restoration and functional rehabilitation, fully implemented the renovation of old residential communities in cities and towns, vigorously promoted urban landscaping and greening, and eliminated pollution. At the same time, it has pushed forward the systematic construction of urban infrastructure, conducted surveys of housing and municipal facilities, and carried out the investigation and rectification of safety hazards, striving to create a high-quality living environment for citizens and make cities more beautiful, safer, and more livable.
by_session.2023-06_01: With the rapid development of China's economy and the steady improvement of people's living standards, urban residents have become increasingly demanding regarding environmental quality and quality of life. Local governments in China are paying more attention to the construction and improvement of public facilities in order to better meet people's needs. By building new squares, parks, and public green spaces, or by redesigning and renovating existing public areas, many cities have provided citizens with more places for leisure and social interaction. Nowadays, fitness equipment purchased by the government and fitness trails paved with public funds can be seen everywhere in many cities, which not only significantly improve the conditions for outdoor activities but also make the cities more beautiful.
by_session.2022-12_03: The Yunnan-Guizhou Plateau, mostly located within the provinces of Yunnan and Guizhou, covers a total area of approximately 500,000 square kilometers with an average altitude of 2,000 to 4,000 meters, making it the fourth largest plateau in China. The plateau slopes downward from west to east and is crisscrossed by numerous rivers, which have formed many deep and steep canyons. In many parts of these canyons, the soil is fertile, which is highly conducive to the growth of various crops. The unique natural environment of the Yunnan-Guizhou Plateau has given rise to biodiversity and cultural diversity. It is a region in China with abundant types of forest and mineral resources, and also an important area for the origin of ancient humans. The Yunnan-Guizhou Plateau is home to the largest number of ethnic minorities in China, each of which has preserved its own rich and colorful cultural traditions.
by_session.2022-12_02: The Loess Plateau, the third largest plateau in China, covers an area of about 600,000 square kilometers, with an average altitude of 1,000 to 2,000 meters. Most of it is covered with loess 50 to 80 meters thick, making it the region with the most concentrated loess distribution and the thickest loess coverage in the world. This is a miracle created by nature, found nowhere else on Earth. The Loess Plateau is one of the cradles of the Chinese nation. As early as 5,500 years ago, people began to engage in farming on the Loess Plateau. With the continuous development of agriculture, the population on the plateau kept increasing, and it became the political and economic center of China during the Qin and Han dynasties. Today, with the implementation of the Western Development Strategy, the economy of the Loess Plateau region has developed rapidly.
by_session.2022-12_01: The Qinghai-Tibet Plateau, located in southwestern China, covers an area of approximately 2.3 million square kilometers with an average altitude of over 4,000 meters, earning it the title of "the roof of the world." It boasts abundant natural resources and picturesque scenery, as well as a variety of rare wild animals. Due to its extremely low temperatures, the plateau has formed a vast expanse of alpine glaciers. As the source of many renowned rivers in Asia, it serves as a major freshwater supplier for China and Southeast Asia. The Qinghai-Tibet Plateau is crucial to the global ecosystem. However, under the influence of climate change, its glaciers are accelerating their melting. China has been making continuous efforts to protect the plateau's ecosystem, resulting in an increasing grassland coverage and more effective conservation of many endangered species.
by_session.2022-06_02: The Nanjing Yangtze River Bridge is the first combined railway and highway bridge over the Yangtze River designed by China and built with domestically produced materials. Its upper deck is a four-lane highway bridge stretching 4,589 meters, while the lower deck is a double-track railway bridge measuring 6,772 meters. The railway bridge connects the former Tianjin-Pukou and Shanghai-Nanjing railway lines, reducing the time for trains to cross the river from one and a half hours in the past to just two minutes today. Serving as a vital hub for north-south transportation, the bridge is also one of Nanjing's renowned scenic spots. The completion of the Nanjing Yangtze River Bridge marked a significant leap in China's bridge construction, greatly facilitating the exchange of goods between the two sides of the Yangtze River.
by_session.2022-06_01: Lugou Bridge is located 15 kilometers southwest of Tiananmen Square, spanning the Yongding River. It is the oldest multi-arch stone bridge still existing in Beijing. Originally built in 1192 and rebuilt in 1698, the bridge is supported by 281 pillars, each topped with a stone lion. On the heads, backs, bellies, or paws of these stone lions are hidden more lions. Vivid in expression and varied in posture, these stone lions are masterpieces of the stone carving art on Lugou Bridge. The stone lions on the bridge are so countless that a popular saying in the Beijing area goes, "The stone lions on Lugou Bridge are beyond counting."
by_session.2021-12_03: Situated at the junction of Hunan and Jiangxi provinces, Jinggangshan is hailed as the "red cradle of the Chinese revolution" for its glorious revolutionary history. In October 1927, veteran revolutionaries such as Mao Zedong and Zhu De led the Chinese Workers' and Peasants' Red Army to this place, where they waged arduous struggles, established the first rural revolutionary base, kindled the spark of the Chinese revolution, and blazed a revolutionary path with Chinese characteristics—"besieging the cities from the countryside and seizing political power by armed force." It was from here that the Chinese revolution marched toward victory. Now, with over 100 revolutionary sites, Jinggangshan has become a "revolutionary history museum without walls," serving as an important base for patriotic education and education in revolutionary traditions.
by_session.2021-12_02: Located in northern Shaanxi Province and along the middle reaches of the Yellow River, Yan'an is the sacred land of the Chinese revolution. Here, Mao Zedong and other veteran revolutionaries lived and fought for thirteen years, leading the War of Resistance against Japanese Aggression and the War of Liberation, cultivating the Yan'an Spirit, and making tremendous contributions to the Chinese revolution. Yan'an boasts the largest number, widest distribution, and highest level of revolutionary sites in the country. It serves as a national base for education in patriotism, revolutionary traditions, and the Yan'an Spirit. With nine revolutionary museums that house a vast collection of important items left by the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China and the veteran revolutionaries during the Yan'an period, Yan'an is renowned as the "City of Chinese Revolutionary Museums."
by_session.2021-12_01: The site of the First National Congress of the Communist Party of China, located at No. 76 Xingye Road in Shanghai, is a typical Shanghai-style residence built in the autumn of 1920. On July 23, 1921, the First National Congress of the Communist Party of China was convened here, during which the Party's first program and first resolution were adopted, the central leadership was elected, and the birth of the Communist Party of China was proclaimed. In September 1952, the site was restored, and a memorial hall was established and opened to the public. In addition to introducing the delegates who attended the First Congress, the memorial hall also presents the Party's historical development process. It has now become a patriotic education base for learning about the Party's history and commemorating revolutionary martyrs.
by_session.2021-06_02: Qinghai Lake is the largest saltwater lake. Known as "China's most beautiful lake," it is one of the most popular tourist attractions and a paradise for photographers and artists. Qinghai boasts magnificent landscapes, vast territory, and abundant resources, with rich reserves of oil and natural gas. Driven by the oil and natural gas industries, the economies of many cities in the province have achieved considerable development. Qinghai is particularly renowned for its abundant water resources, serving as the source of China's three major rivers—the Yangtze, the Yellow, and the Lancang—and playing a vital role in the country's water ecology.
by_session.2021-06_03: Yunnan is a province in southwest China, with an average elevation of 1,500 meters. It has a long history, beautiful scenery, and a pleasant climate. With its superior ecological environment and rich biodiversity, Yunnan is renowned as a paradise for wildlife. The province also boasts various mineral deposits and abundant water resources, which provide favorable conditions for the sustainable development of its economy. Yunnan is home to 25 ethnic minorities, most of whom have their own languages, customs, and religions. The unique natural scenery and rich ethnic culture make Yunnan one of the most popular tourist destinations in China, attracting a large number of domestic and international tourists every year.
by_session.2021-06_01: Hainan, with abundant sunshine, rich biodiversity, dense hot springs, and crystal-clear seawater, has most of its beaches ideal for swimming and sunbathing almost all year round, thus it is hailed as China's "Garden of Four Seasons" and a holiday resort, attracting a large number of domestic and foreign tourists every year. Since its establishment as a province in 1988, Hainan has witnessed rapid development in tourism, service industries, and high-tech industries, and it is the only provincial-level special economic zone in China. With strong support from the central government and the people nationwide, Hainan will be built into China's largest pilot free trade zone.
by_session.2020-12_03: Beijing Daxing International Airport, located 46 kilometers south of Tiananmen Square, was put into operation on September 30, 2019. This mega project started construction in 2014, with over 40,000 workers at its peak. The terminal building is compactly designed to allow the maximum number of aircraft to park directly at the positions closest to the center of the terminal, providing great convenience for passengers. The terminal has 82 gates, but it takes passengers less than 8 minutes to reach any gate after passing through security check. The airport's design ensures 300 takeoffs and landings per hour. Its annual passenger volume is expected to reach 100 million by 2040, making it likely to become the busiest airport in the world.
by_session.2020-09_02: Dream of the Red Chamber is a novel written by Cao Xueqin in the 18th century. Based on his own painful personal experiences, Cao Xueqin tells the tragic love story between Jia Baoyu and Lin Daiyu. The book features approximately 30 major characters and over 400 minor ones, each vividly portrayed with distinct personalities. The novel meticulously depicts the rise and fall of four noble families, reflecting the hidden crises and intricate social conflicts within feudal society. Blending realism and romanticism, Dream of the Red Chamber possesses strong artistic appeal. It is widely regarded as the greatest Chinese novel and one of the finest literary works in the world.
by_session.2020-12_01: Beijing Daxing International Airport, located 46 kilometers south of Tiananmen Square, was put into operation on September 30, 2019. Construction of this mega project started in 2014, with over 40,000 workers at the construction site during peak times. The terminal has a compact design, allowing the maximum number of aircraft to park directly at the positions closest to the center of the terminal, which provides great convenience for passengers. With 82 boarding gates in total, passengers can reach any gate in less than 8 minutes after passing through security checks. The airport is designed to ensure 300 takeoffs and landings per hour. Its annual passenger throughput is expected to reach 100 million by 2040, making it likely to become the busiest airport in the world.
by_session.2020-09_01: Journey to the West is perhaps the most influential of the four great classical novels in Chinese literature, and certainly the best-known one abroad. This novel depicts the arduous journey of the famous monk Xuanzang, accompanied by three disciples, traveling across the western regions of China to India to obtain Buddhist scriptures. Although the theme of the story is based on Buddhism, the novel draws heavily on Chinese folk tales and myths, creating various vivid characters and animal images. The most famous among them is Sun Wukong, whose stories of fighting against various demons and monsters are known to almost every Chinese child.
by_session.2020-07_01: The Romance of the Three Kingdoms is a famous Chinese historical novel written in the 14th century. Set against the historical background of the Three Kingdoms period, it depicts the wars among the three kingdoms of Wei, Shu, and Wu, spanning from the latter half of the 2nd century AD to the latter half of the 3rd century AD. The novel portrays nearly a thousand characters and countless historical events. Although most of these characters and events are based on real history, they have been romanticized and dramatized to varying degrees. The Romance of the Three Kingdoms is universally acknowledged as a literary masterpiece. Since its publication, it has continuously attracted generations of readers and exerted a broad and lasting influence on Chinese culture.
by_session.2019-12_01: Peonies, with their bright colors and elegant appearance, symbolize peace and prosperity, thus they are known as the "king of flowers" in China. Peonies are cultivated and planted in many parts of China. Over the centuries, numerous poems and paintings have been created to praise peonies. During the Tang Dynasty, peonies were widely planted in royal gardens and were honored as the national flower, which made them particularly popular. In the tenth century, the ancient city of Luoyang became the center of peony cultivation, and this status has been maintained to this day. Nowadays, thousands of domestic tourists flock to Luoyang for the annual Peony Festival to appreciate the unique beauty of Luoyang peonies while exploring the history of the ancient capital of nine dynasties.
by_session.2019-06_03: Chinese idioms are a unique form of expression in Chinese, mostly composed of four Chinese characters. They are highly concise and fixed in form, but can often vividly convey profound meanings. Most Chinese idioms originate from ancient Chinese literary works and are usually associated with certain myths, legends, or historical events. Without knowledge of the origin of a particular idiom, it is difficult to grasp its exact meaning. Therefore, learning idioms helps people better understand traditional Chinese culture. Chinese idioms are widely used in daily conversation and literary creation. Using idioms appropriately can make one's language more expressive and communication more effective.
by_session.2019-06_01: China is a vast country with a large population, and people in many places speak their own dialects. Dialects vary greatly in pronunciation, while differences in vocabulary and grammar are relatively minor. Some dialects, especially those in the north and south, differ so greatly that speakers of different dialects often find it difficult to understand each other. Dialects are regarded as an integral part of local culture, but in recent years the number of people who can speak them has been declining. To encourage people to speak their local languages more, some local governments have taken measures such as offering dialect classes in schools and broadcasting dialect programs on radio and television, in the hope of preserving local cultural heritage.
by_session.2019-06_02: Chinese is now the language with the largest number of native speakers in the world. An important difference between Chinese and Western languages lies in that it is composed of characters rather than letters. Among the writing systems still in use, Chinese is the oldest. In China, people from different regions may not understand each other's dialects, but due to the unified written form of Chinese characters, they can communicate with almost no difficulty. Historically, the Chinese language has played a vital role in uniting the Chinese nation. Today, with China's rapid economic growth and increasing global influence, more and more people from other countries are beginning to learn Chinese.
by_session.2018-12_03: In recent years, the Chinese government has further increased investment in the construction of stadiums and gymnasiums to better meet the rapidly growing fitness needs of the people. In addition to building new venues, many cities have taken measures such as renovating old factories and commercial buildings to increase the number of local sports facilities. With government financial support, more and more stadiums and gymnasiums are opening to the public for free or charging only a modest fee. By applying modern information technology, many venues have significantly improved the quality of their services. People can conveniently book venues and make payments online. It is foreseeable that, with the continuous improvement of sports facilities, an increasing number of people will go to stadiums and gymnasiums for exercise.
by_session.2018-06_03: Bicycles were once the primary mode of transportation in both urban and rural China, and the country was known as the "Kingdom of Bicycles." Nowadays, with the increasing severity of urban traffic congestion and air pollution, cycling has regained popularity. Recently, Chinese entrepreneurs have combined mobile internet technology with traditional bicycles, creating a business model called shared bikes. The advent of shared bikes has made cycling more convenient, as people can use them anytime with just a smartphone. To encourage cycling, many cities have built dedicated bicycle lanes. Now, more and more Chinese people also enjoy cycling for fitness.
by_session.2018-12_02: China is paying increasing attention to public libraries and encourages people to make full use of them. Recently released statistics show that the number of public libraries in China has been increasing year by year. Many libraries have created a quieter and more comfortable environment for readers through renovation and expansion. Large public libraries not only provide a wide variety of reference materials but also regularly hold lectures, exhibitions, and other events. In recent years, many digital libraries have emerged, thus saving the space needed for storing books. Some libraries have also introduced self-service systems, making it more convenient for readers to borrow and return books, and further meeting the needs of readers.
by_session.2018-12_01: In recent years, an increasing number of museums in China have been open to the public free of charge. Both the number of exhibitions and the number of visitors have increased significantly. Long queues have become a common sight in front of some popular museums, which have to take measures to limit the number of visitors. Nowadays, exhibition forms are becoming increasingly diverse. Some large museums utilize advanced technologies such as multimedia and virtual reality to make their exhibitions more appealing. Many museums also hold online exhibitions, allowing people to view rare exhibits on the Internet. However, for most visitors, the experience of viewing exhibits on site remains more attractive.
by_session.2018-06_02: China currently possesses the world's largest and fastest high-speed railway network. The operating speed of high-speed trains will continue to increase, and more cities will build high-speed rail stations. High-speed rail has greatly shortened people's travel time. Compared with airplanes, the outstanding advantage of high-speed trains lies in their punctuality, as they are basically unaffected by weather or traffic control. High-speed rail has significantly transformed the lifestyle of the Chinese people. Nowadays, it has become the preferred mode of transportation for many people on business trips. More and more people also choose to travel by high-speed rail during holidays. In addition, many young people opt to work in one city while living in a neighboring city, commuting by high-speed rail every day.
by_session.2018-06_01: In the past, owning a private car was a luxury for most Chinese people. Nowadays, private cars can be seen everywhere in China. Cars have become an indispensable part of people’s lives, as they not only drive to work but also frequently go on road trips. The growth of cars in some cities is so rapid that traffic congestion and insufficient parking spaces are becoming increasingly severe, prompting the municipal governments of these cities to introduce new regulations restricting the number of cars on the road. Due to increasingly serious air pollution, more and more people are now choosing to purchase new energy vehicles, and the Chinese government has also taken measures to support their development.
by_session.2017-12_03: Dongting Lake, located in the northeastern part of Hunan Province, covers a vast area but is quite shallow. It serves as a flood storage basin for the Yangtze River, and its size largely depends on seasonal changes. The two provinces, Hubei and Hunan, take their names from their relative positions to the lake, with Hubei meaning "north of the lake" and Hunan meaning "south of the lake." As the birthplace of the dragon boat race, Dongting Lake enjoys a prestigious status in Chinese culture. Legend has it that the dragon boat race originated on the eastern shore of Dongting Lake as an effort to search for the remains of Qu Yuan, the patriotic poet of the State of Chu. Every year, the dragon boat race, together with the enchanting scenery of Dongting Lake and its surrounding areas, attracts tens of thousands of tourists from all corners of the country and around the world.
by_session.2017-12_02: Qinghai Lake, situated at an altitude of 3,205 meters and about 100 kilometers west of Xining, the capital of Qinghai Province, is the largest saltwater lake in China, with an area of 4,317 square kilometers and a maximum depth of 25.5 meters. It receives water from 23 rivers, most of which are seasonal. Eighty percent of the lake's water comes from five major rivers. Qinghai Lake lies at the intersection of several migratory bird routes across Asia. Many birds use it as a resting place during their migration. On the western side of the lake is the famous “Bird Island,” which attracts birdwatchers from all over the world. Every summer, tourists also come here to watch the international cycling race.
by_session.2017-06_03: The Tang Dynasty, which began in 618 and ended in 907, was the most brilliant period in Chinese history. After nearly 300 years of development, Tang China became the most prosperous and powerful country in the world, with its capital Chang'an being the largest metropolis of the time. During this period, the economy was developed, commerce flourished, and social order was stable, with even the border areas being open to the outside world. As urbanization and wealth increased, art and literature also thrived. Li Bai and Du Fu were poets renowned for the simplicity and naturalness of their works. Their poems touched the hearts of both scholars and common people. Even today, many of their poems are still widely read and recited by children and adults alike.
by_session.2017-12_01: Taihu Lake is a freshwater lake in eastern China, covering an area of 2,250 square kilometers. It is the third largest freshwater lake in China, after Poyang and Dongting. Taihu Lake has about 90 islands, ranging in size from a few square meters to several square kilometers. Taihu Lake is famous for its unique "Taihu stones", which are often used to decorate traditional Chinese gardens. Taihu Lake is also known for its productive fishing industry. Since the late 1970s, fishing for fish and crabs has been extremely important for residents along the lake, and has made significant contributions to the economy of the surrounding areas. The Taihu Lake region is one of the bases of China's ceramics industry, where the ceramic factories in Yixing produce the world-famous Yixing clay teapots.
by_session.2017-06_02: The Song Dynasty began in 960 and lasted until 1279. During this period, China's economy grew substantially, making it the most advanced economy in the world, with flourishing science, technology, philosophy, and mathematics. The Song Dynasty was the first in world history to issue paper currency. It also first used gunpowder and invented movable-type printing. The population grew rapidly, and more and more people lived in cities, where there were bustling entertainment venues. Social life was diverse. People gathered to view and trade precious artworks. The government system of the Song Dynasty was also advanced for its time. Government officials were selected and appointed through competitive examinations.
by_session.2017-06_01: The Ming Dynasty ruled China for 276 years and is depicted as one of the greatest eras of orderly governance and social stability in human history. During this period, the development of handicrafts promoted a market economy and urbanization. A wide variety of goods, including wine and silk, were sold in the markets. Meanwhile, many foreign commodities, such as clocks and tobacco, were imported. Major commercial centers like Beijing, Nanjing, Yangzhou, and Suzhou emerged one after another. It was also during the Ming Dynasty that the fleet led by Zheng He made seven large-scale exploratory voyages to the Indian Ocean. It is also worth mentioning that three of the four great classic novels of Chinese literature were written in the Ming Dynasty.
by_session.2016-12_03: Agriculture is an important industry in China, employing over 300 million people. China ranks first in the world in agricultural output, mainly producing rice, wheat, and beans. Although China's agricultural land accounts for only ten percent of the world's total, it provides food for twenty percent of the global population. Rice cultivation in China began 7,700 years ago. Long before the use of machinery and chemical fertilizers, hardworking and innovative Chinese farmers had already adopted a variety of methods to increase crop yields. The latest development in China's agriculture is the promotion of organic farming, which can serve multiple purposes simultaneously, including food safety, public health, and sustainable development.
by_session.2016-12_01: With the improvement of living standards, vacationing is playing an increasingly important role in the lives of Chinese people. In the past, Chinese people mainly spent their time making a living and rarely had the opportunity to travel. However, in recent years, China’s tourism industry has developed rapidly. The economic boom and the emergence of a wealthy middle class have triggered an unprecedented tourism boom. Chinese people are not only traveling within the country, but outbound travel has also become increasingly common. During the National Day holiday in 2016, total tourism consumption exceeded 400 billion yuan. According to the estimation of the World Trade Organization, China will become the world's largest tourism country by 2020, and it will be the fastest-growing country in outbound tourism expenditure in the coming years.
by_session.2016-06_03: Shenzhen is a newly developed city in Guangdong Province, China. Before the reform and opening-up, it was nothing but a fishing village with a population of just over 30,000. In the 1980s, the Chinese government established the Shenzhen Special Economic Zone as an experimental field for implementing the socialist market economy. Today, Shenzhen has a population of over 10 million, and the whole city has undergone tremendous changes. By 2014, its per-capita GDP had reached 25,000 US dollars, equivalent to the level of some developed countries. In terms of comprehensive economic strength, Shenzhen ranks among the top cities in China. Due to its unique status, it is also an ideal place for entrepreneurs from home and abroad to start their businesses.
by_session.2016-06_01: China's innovation is booming at an unprecedented pace. In order to catch up with and surpass developed countries in science and technology as soon as possible, China has substantially increased its research and development funding in recent years. Chinese universities and research institutes are actively conducting innovative research, covering high-tech fields ranging from big data to biochemistry, and from new energy to robotics. They also collaborate with science and technology parks across the country to commercialize their innovations. Meanwhile, whether in products or business models, Chinese entrepreneurs are striving to be pioneers of innovation, in order to meet the ever-changing and growing demands of both domestic and international consumer markets.
by_session.2016-06_02: Qipao is an elegant Chinese garment that originates from the Manchu Nationality of China. During the Qing Dynasty, it was a loose robe worn by royal women. In the 1920s, influenced by Western clothing, some changes took place in qipao. The cuffs became narrower and the robe length shorter. These changes fully revealed the beauty of women. Today, qipao frequently appears on world-class fashion shows. When Chinese women attend important social gatherings, qipao is often their first choice. Many Chinese brides also choose qipao as their wedding dress. Some influential people even suggest that qipao should be designated as the national costume for Chinese women.
by_session.2016-12_02: With the vigorous development of China's economy, the number of people learning Chinese has increased rapidly, making Chinese one of the most sought-after languages in the world. In recent years, Chinese universities have also seen a significant rise in their international rankings. Thanks to the remarkable progress in China's education, it is no surprise that China has become one of the most popular study destinations for overseas students. In 2015, nearly 400,000 international students flocked to the Chinese market. The subjects they study are no longer limited to Chinese language and culture, but also include science and engineering. In the global education market, the United States and the United Kingdom still dominate, but China is rapidly catching up.
by_session.2019-12_03: The lotus flower is one of China's famous flowers and is deeply loved by people. Lakes and ponds in many parts of China are suitable for the growth of lotus flowers. Lotus flowers are brightly colored, blooming in the early morning in summer and closing at night. The flowering period lasts for two to three months, attracting tourists from all over to come and admire them. Lotus flowers have multiple functions: they can not only green the water surface and beautify gardens, but also purify water, reduce pollution, and improve the environment. Lotus flowers face the scorching sun without fear and emerge from mud unstained, symbolizing purity and elegance. They are often used to metaphorically represent people's noble virtues and have always been an important subject for poets and painters. Places where lotus flowers are in full bloom are also frequented by many photography enthusiasts.
